mapping.a_element: accuracy threshold separating correctable from uncorrectable quantum computation
mapping.b_element: multicritical point p_c separating the ordered (ferromagnetic) from the disordered spin-glass phase

mapping.a_element: noise/error rate in the biased CPHASE gates
mapping.b_element: disorder probability p of the +/-J bonds in the Ising model

brief_justification: Both texts feature a critical threshold separating a working/ordered regime from a failing/disordered one, but the machinery used to obtain it differs: paper A derives rigorous lower bounds on the accuracy threshold via circuit-level fault-tolerant error-correction analysis exploiting noise asymmetry, whereas paper B locates the multicritical point through a duality relation between two partition functions plus the replica method. The overlap is the generic notion of a critical threshold, not a shared role-for-role governing process, so no coherent mapping of A's fault-tolerance machinery onto B's duality/replica statistical-mechanics machinery holds from the given abstracts.
